Students must request a registration override from the instructor to enroll in this course. Prerequisite(s): A 2.5 cumulative average in Psychology.

Non-Ordinary Consciousness, Meditation, Psychedelics and the Brain: This course explores how non-ordinary states of consciousness induced by meditation and psychedelics can transform the brain and enhance well-being. We examine meditation practices and psychedelic substances (such as psilocybin and MDMA) as distinct pathways to altered states that show promise for improving cognitive and emotional function and for treating disorders including addiction, depression, anxiety, and PTSD. Students will evaluate the latest psychological and neuroscience research on therapeutic potential, neural mechanisms, and risks of these interventions. We will also address ethical, legal, policy, and social implications, as well as challenges and opportunities for future developments in this rapidly advancing field. Prerequisites: PSY 2100 or PSY 2050 and junior/senior standing or permission of the instructor. Prerequisites: PSY 2100 and junior/senior standing.

This course addresses the psychological, biological, cultural, and ethical issues of human sexuality. Topics will include female and male sexual anatomy, love, commitment, sexual orientation, sexually transmitted infections, contraception and abortion, pregnancy and childbirth, sexual dysfunction, sexual deviation, sexual coercion, and commercial sex. Prerequisites: PSY 2100 and junior/senior standing or permission of the instructor.
Students must request a registration override from the instructor to enroll in this course. This is a variable credit course that students may take for 3 to 12 credits.
